mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the effective federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and eases financial conditions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection in labor market slack that leads equity markets to price in weaker consumption and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC conditional on trigger is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and residential investment demand strength that compounds into higher corporate earnings over a quarterly horizon, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. Neither series alone is sufficient; both must be rising together to confirm durable demand momentum.
failure_condition: The joint-trigger signal underperforms the single-series RSAFS-only signal in out-of-sample Rank IC, or 60-day forward returns show no significant difference (p > 0.10) between joint-positive and single-positive states.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum that markets incorporate quickly, generating a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as short-term trend followers react to the print.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return following positive INDPRO month-over-month prints is not significantly different from average return following negative prints (p > 0.10) across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, when the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% while headline CPI remains elevated, the combination signals the central bank will maintain restrictive policy longer than markets price, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The effect requires both high rates and high inflation jointly; either alone is insufficient because low inflation would permit a dovish pivot.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the joint condition produces no Rank IC improvement over FEDFUNDS-only signal in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth below a 100,000 3-month average signals the labor market is turning, foreshadowing a broader slowdown that equity markets underprice initially, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following the payroll deceleration trigger during late-cycle periods are not significantly lower than unconditional 60-day returns (p > 0.10), or the effect reverses sign in more than one out of three tested economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts of more than 10% from their trailing 6-month high signals deteriorating interest-rate-sensitive demand and forward-looking consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets reassess growth expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following the HOUST drawdown trigger are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC conditional on trigger is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in industrial production growth and payroll employment growth signals a coordinated weakening of supply-side output and labor demand that historically precedes broader economic downturns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. The interaction is required because either series weakening alone commonly reflects noise or sector-specific shocks rather than a systemic slowdown.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth < 0 AND PAYEMS 3-month average change < 50,000
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during joint-trigger periods are not significantly lower than during single-series-only weakening periods (p > 0.10), or the joint signal fails to outperform the INDPRO-only baseline in walk-forward Rank IC.
